claim: API discovery using vector retrieval alone misses business process context; agents need knowledge graphs to identify prerequisite APIs that are not semantically similar to the user query
actionable: Combine vector retrieval with knowledge graph-based process discovery to ensure agents find all required APIs including prerequisites (e.g., create purchase requisition before purchase order)
source_title: AI Dev 25 x NYC | Christoph Meyer, Lars Heling: Improving AI Agent Discovery with a Knowledge Graph

claim: Knowledge graphs embed API specifications using textual descriptions of nodes and their connected nodes, creating richer embeddings than simple metadata alone
actionable: Generate embedding text for APIs by concatenating their name, properties, relationships, and connected entities from the knowledge graph
source_title: AI Dev 25 x NYC | Christoph Meyer, Lars Heling: Improving AI Agent Discovery with a Knowledge Graph

claim: Domain-specific terminology (acronyms, abbreviations with multiple meanings like PO for product owner vs. purchase order) requires ontology enrichment in knowledge graphs to disambiguate for agents
actionable: Add explicit mappings in the knowledge graph from natural language descriptions (active) to technical IDs (02) to help agents construct correct API calls
source_title: AI Dev 25 x NYC | Christoph Meyer, Lars Heling: Improving AI Agent Discovery with a Knowledge Graph

claim: A three-tool agent architecture (discover API, retrieve API spec, execute API) scaled with a knowledge graph can handle thousands of APIs without creating one tool per API
actionable: Implement broad tools that use knowledge graph queries for discovery and specification retrieval; avoid per-API tools that create tool overload
source_title: AI Dev 25 x NYC | Christoph Meyer, Lars Heling: Improving AI Agent Discovery with a Knowledge Graph

claim: Not all metadata is readily AI-ready; missing descriptions and overly technical IDs require enrichment before agents can use them effectively
actionable: Audit your knowledge graph for gaps in descriptions and context; add human-readable mappings for technical IDs and abbreviations
source_title: AI Dev 25 x NYC | Christoph Meyer, Lars Heling: Improving AI Agent Discovery with a Knowledge Graph

claim: Knowledge graph subgraphs can scope discovery and retrieval differently; use larger graphs for embeddings but smaller subgraphs for API specification retrieval to manage complexity
actionable: Segment your knowledge graph by application domain; use full graph for discovery embeddings but serve agents only relevant subgraphs during execution
source_title: AI Dev 25 x NYC | Christoph Meyer, Lars Heling: Improving AI Agent Discovery with a Knowledge Graph

claim: Process information (business process modeling, BPMN, Arato workflows) encoded in a knowledge graph is essential for agents to determine the correct order of API calls
actionable: Encode business processes in your knowledge graph using edges that represent process sequences, then query these edges during agent planning
source_title: AI Dev 25 x NYC | Christoph Meyer, Lars Heling: Improving AI Agent Discovery with a Knowledge Graph
